data_in.description: Untargeted metabolomics feature table (m/z, retention time, intensity across samples)

data_out.description: Functional activity prediction report mapping features to metabolic network modules and pathways

domain_knowledge: Mummichog bypasses metabolite identification by directly leveraging metabolic network organization, which allows functional prediction from feature tables alone without compound-level annotation.
Feature mapping to metabolic networks requires m/z tolerance windows appropriate for the mass spectrometry instrument used (typically ±5–10 ppm for high-resolution instruments).

Metabolic networks in Mummichog are organized as graphs where nodes represent metabolites and edges represent biochemical transformations, enabling signal propagation across connected metabolic modules.
Functional activity prediction relies on aggregating signal evidence from multiple features mapping to a shared metabolic pathway or module, rather than identification of individual metabolites.

direct_checks: verify that the mummichog package can be loaded from metabolomics-cloud/mummichog GitHub repository
verify that the core pipeline accepts a feature table file (CSV or tabular format) as input
verify that the pipeline outputs a structured result containing functional activity predictions
verify that the pipeline executes without requiring a metabolite identification step as an obligatory prerequisite
verify that the pipeline produces network mapping artifacts (e.g., feature-to-reaction or feature-to-pathway assignments)
script_runs: execute the main pipeline entry point on a provided test feature table and confirm non-error exit
file_format_is: output artifact contains structured predictions (JSON, TSV, or similar); no canonical answer for exact output format without specification
expert_review: assess whether the fixed architecture layer correctly implements the intended metabolic network topology and mass-neutral loss propagation logic
assess whether functional activity predictions are scientifically plausible given the input feature masses and network structure
assess whether the bypass of metabolite identification genuinely preserves predictive power or introduces unacceptable bias
evaluate the reproducibility and robustness of network mapping results across different feature table subsets

expected_outputs: Functional activity prediction report mapping features to metabolic network modules and pathways
finding: Mummichog is a Python program that leverages the organization of metabolic networks to predict functional activity directly from feature tables, bypassing metabolite identification.
inputs: Untargeted metabolomics feature table (m/z, retention time, intensity across samples)
methodology_summary: Load feature table with m/z, retention time, and sample intensity data.
Initialize metabolic network database and retrieve network structure (nodes = metabolites, edges = reactions).
Map feature m/z values to metabolic network nodes using mass-to-charge tolerance matching.
Propagate feature signal intensity through connected network pathways to infer metabolic module activity.
Aggregate network-level activity signals and generate functional predictions without explicit metabolite identification.
Validation: Confirm functional activity predictions are generated and network mapping statistics show connectivity across multiple metabolic modules.

task_description: Implement the Mummichog 3 core pipeline to predict functional activity from an untargeted metabolomics feature table by mapping features onto metabolic networks without requiring metabolite identification. Output a functional activity prediction report.
task_objective: Implement the Mummichog 3 pipeline that accepts a metabolomics feature table and outputs predicted functional activity by mapping features to metabolic networks without requiring metabolite identification.

task_outputs.label: Functional activity predictions and network mapping results

title: Reconstruct the metabolic network-based feature-table analysis pipeline in Mummichog 3

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: Python.
workflow_description: 1. Load the untargeted metabolomics feature table (CSV/TSV format with m/z and retention time columns). 2. Initialize the metabolic network database and retrieve the network structure. 3. Map feature m/z values to metabolic network nodes using mass-to-charge tolerance matching. 4. Propagate feature signals through the metabolic network to infer functional modules and pathways. 5. Aggregate network-level activity scores and generate functional activity predictions without performing explicit metabolite identification. 6. Output functional activity predictions and network mapping statistics.
